subject: SCIENCE
title: Care for Our Shared Home
introduction: Reduce waste, protect habitat, conserve water, and observe wildlife without disturbance. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Care for Our Shared Home”.
A key word used in the lesson “Care for Our Shared Home”.
A key word used in the lesson “Care for Our Shared Home”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: Reduce waste, protect habitat, conserve water, and observe wildlife without disturbance.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: Reduce waste, protect habitat, conserve water, and observe wildlife without disturbance.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

feedback.correct: Correct: Leave a bird nest undisturbed.
feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. Take eggs home. and Break branches for fun. do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Care for Our Shared Home”?

options.text: Reduce waste, protect habitat, conserve water, and observe wildlife without disturbance.

items.explanation: The correct response is “Reduce waste, protect habitat, conserve water, and observe wildlife without disturbance.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Reduce waste, protect habitat, conserve water, and observe wildlife without disturbance.
The correct response is “use reusable container” because it follows the lesson explanation: Reduce waste, protect habitat, conserve water, and observe wildlife without disturbance.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?
items.explanation: The correct response is “Release pets into wild” because it follows the lesson explanation: Reduce waste, protect habitat, conserve water, and observe wildlife without disturbance.
The correct response is “Leave a bird nest undisturbed.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Reduce waste, protect habitat, conserve water, and observe wildlife without disturbance.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?
items.explanation: The correct response is “leave nests undisturbed” because it follows the lesson explanation: Reduce waste, protect habitat, conserve water, and observe wildlife without disturbance.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Care for Our Shared Home; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Care for Our Shared Home.

safetyNotes: Use the on-screen activity only; no unsupervised physical experiment is required.
